page: The UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of a 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. Storage is bundled as a 200 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU is present.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is highly efficient, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU score fall into the average category. Memory subsystem performance is a notable bottleneck, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the weak category (bottom 25%), although raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks maintain average tier results.

With a vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1:2, this server is positioned as a compute-oriented configuration. The shared CPU allocation model introduces potential resource contention, which aligns with its average multi-core benchmark results. The instance demonstrates average performance for database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Large language model inference is highly constrained, with medium-sized model text generation dropping to the poor category (bottom 10%). Consequently, this configuration is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and development workloads that benefit from strong single-core burst capabilities but do not require sustained, high-density multi-core execution or massive memory bandwidth.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 200 GB local SSD, and no GPU. Single-core benchmarks, including stress-ng and single-threaded compression, rank in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core performance and database operations fall into the average category, while PassMark memory scores are weak. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation make this instance suitable for compute-focused workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution speed over high-density multi-core processing or intensive memory bandwidth, such as lightweight web serving, single-threaded data processing, and basic development environments.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance but average multi-core and weak memory results.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An AMD EPYC virtual server featuring top-tier single-core processing speed, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB local SSD.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with x86_64 architecture
16.0 GB of system memory and a 200 GB local SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance
Average multi-core CPU, Redis, and web serving throughput
Weak memory performance and limited medium-model LLM inference
Suited for single-threaded processing and lightweight web servers
